title: DSDS Use Cases Definition
description: Documents what an artifact is used for — when it is the right choice and when a different artifact fits better. An optional `purpose` statement gives the umbrella answer; `items` holds scenario-driven use cases, each marked 'recommended' or 'discouraged'.

useCase.description: A single use case scenario describing when an artifact or option is or is not appropriate.

description.description: Describes the scenario. Should be concrete and situation-driven — describe the user's situation, not the artifact's features.

stance.description: Whether this scenario is one to follow ('recommended') or to avoid ('discouraged').

alternative.description: The recommended alternative artifact for this scenario. Typically provided for discouraged scenarios to direct the reader toward a more appropriate component, pattern, or token.

identifier.description: The identifier of the recommended alternative artifact (e.g., 'link', 'radio-group', 'icon-button', 'empty-state').

rationale.description: Why the alternative is more appropriate for this scenario. Cite semantic differences, accessibility implications, or UX rationale when available.

useCases.description: Documents what an artifact is used for — when it is the right choice and when to choose something else. The optional `purpose` statement is the umbrella answer: a short summary of what the artifact is for, framing the use cases beneath it. `items` holds scenario-driven `useCase` entries, each marked 'recommended' (when to use) or 'discouraged' (when to choose an alternative). Use `useCases` to answer *whether* to use the artifact; for *how* to use it correctly, see `guidelines`; for the higher-level beliefs that shape a domain, see `principles`; for *what* it looks like and how it behaves, see variants, states, and anatomy.

kind.description: Identifies this document block as a use-cases spec.

purpose.description: The umbrella purpose statement for the artifact — one or two sentences saying what it is for, framing the use cases in `items` (e.g., 'Buttons trigger immediate actions. They are the primary mechanism for initiating changes within a surface.').

items.description: Scenario-driven use cases describing when this artifact is or is not the right choice. Order matters: tools SHOULD preserve it for display. Authors SHOULD lead with recommended scenarios.
